privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real forensic test data downloaded from the DFTT project (NTFS Undelete test image). I verified that all target case attributes, disk image paths, and report paths strictly match the task description and verifier logic. However, I discovered two critical flaws in the evaluation logic. First, the ground truth generation script (setup_task.sh) uses a broken substring search (if " r/r " in line or " -/r " in line) to parse `fls` output, which permanently skips all files located in the root directory (as they begin directly with `r/r` without a preceding space). Consequently, the verifier rigidly expects incorrect counts: TOTAL_FILES = 8 (instead of ~27) and DELETED_FILES = 3 (instead of 8+). An agent accurately reporting the true file counts from Autopsy will fail the verifier strict +/- 5 tolerance. Second, the verifier expects LARGEST_FILE_NAME to be tracking.log by explicitly ignoring all system files (via name NOT LIKE "$%" in its SQL query), even though the task instructions simply ask for the "largest file" without specifying to exclude NTFS system metadata like $BadClus:$Bad (6 MB) or $LogFile (2 MB).
